COVER.precedent_template:
DRAFT RED HERRING PROSPECTUS
Dated: [CURRENT_DATE]
Please read Section 32 of the Companies Act, 2013

100% BOOK BUILT ISSUE

[COMPANY_NAME] (the "Company" or "Issuer") was incorporated under the Companies Act with CIN: [CIN]. Registered Office: [REGISTERED_OFFICE].

PUBLIC ISSUE OF UP TO [FRESH_ISSUE_SHARES] EQUITY SHARES OF FACE VALUE OF ₹[FACE_VALUE] EACH OF [COMPANY_NAME] FOR CASH AT A PRICE OF ₹[PRICE_BAND_HIGH] PER EQUITY SHARE AGGREGATING UP TO ₹[ISSUE_SIZE_CR] CRORES ("THE ISSUE") ON THE SME PLATFORM OF THE STOCK EXCHANGE.

RISK IN RELATION TO THE FIRST ISSUE: This being the first public issue of Equity Shares of our Company, there has been no formal market for the Equity Shares. The face value of the Equity Shares is ₹[FACE_VALUE] and the Floor Price is 3 times of the face value and the Cap Price is 5 times of the face value.

LEAD MANAGER TO THE ISSUE: Merchant Banker (Lead Manager)
REGISTRAR TO THE ISSUE: Share Transfer Agent
keywords: RISK IN RELATION TO THE FIRST ISSUE

RISK.precedent_template:
SECTION II: RISK FACTORS

An investment in equity shares involves a high degree of risk. Prospective investors should carefully consider all the information in this Draft Red Herring Prospectus, including the risks and uncertainties described below, before making an investment decision in our Equity Shares.

1. BUSINESS & OPERATIONAL RISKS:
Our business is subject to rapid technological and market changes in our industry. Any failure to innovate or meet client demands could adversely affect our revenues. In FY3, our operating revenue stood at ₹[REVENUE_FY3] Crores with an EBITDA of ₹[EBITDA_FY3] Crores.

2. CLIENT CONCENTRATION RISK:
A significant portion of our revenues is derived from a limited number of top clients. The loss of any major client or a significant reduction in business from them could materially impair our financial condition.

3. SME EXCHANGE LIQUIDITY RISK:
The Equity Shares of our Company are proposed to be listed on the SME Platform of the Stock Exchange. Trading on the SME Platform involves a minimum lot size of ₹2,00,000 (per SEBI ICDR 2025 norms), which may limit secondary market liquidity compared to the Main Board.

4. WORKING CAPITAL & CASH FLOW RISKS:
Our business requires substantial working capital. Any delay in realization of receivables or tightening of credit facilities from banks could impact our day-to-day operations.

OBJECTS.precedent_template:
SECTION IV: OBJECTS OF THE ISSUE

The Net Proceeds from the Fresh Issue of Equity Shares aggregating up to ₹[ISSUE_SIZE_CR] Crores are proposed to be utilized in accordance with Regulation 230 of the SEBI (ICDR) Regulations towards the following objects:

1. Funding Capital Expenditure Requirements: Towards expansion of our operating infrastructure and technology upgrade.
2. Working Capital Requirements: To support our expanding scale of operations and growing order book.
3. General Corporate Purposes (GCP): Subject to Regulation 230(2), the allocation towards General Corporate Purposes shall not exceed 15% of the total issue size or ₹10.00 Crores, whichever is lower.

SCHEDULE OF IMPLEMENTATION & FUND DEPLOYMENT:
The entire Net Proceeds are expected to be deployed across FY 2026-27 and FY 2027-28 as monitored by our Audit Committee.

CAPITAL.precedent_template:
SECTION III: CAPITAL STRUCTURE

The share capital of our Company prior to the Issue and as proposed post-Issue is set forth below:

1. AUTHORIZED SHARE CAPITAL:
5,00,00,000 Equity Shares of Face Value ₹[FACE_VALUE]/- each aggregating to ₹50.00 Crores.

2. ISSUED, SUBSCRIBED & PAID-UP SHARE CAPITAL PRIOR TO THE ISSUE:
[PRE_ISSUE_SHARES] Equity Shares of ₹[FACE_VALUE]/- each.

3. PRESENT ISSUE IN TERMS OF THIS DRHP:
Public Issue of up to [FRESH_ISSUE_SHARES] Fresh Equity Shares plus an Offer for Sale (OFS) of up to [OFS_SHARES] Equity Shares (OFS capped at max 20% of total issue per Regulation 230(1)).

4. PAID-UP SHARE CAPITAL AFTER THE ISSUE:
Post-Issue Paid-up Share Capital will stand at ₹[POST_ISSUE_PAID_UP_CAPITAL] Crores (satisfying Regulation 229 SME corridor of ₹1 Crore to ₹25 Crore).

BIZ_OVERVIEW.precedent_template:
SECTION VI: OUR BUSINESS & INDUSTRY OVERVIEW

1. OVERVIEW OF OUR BUSINESS:
[COMPANY_NAME] is a fast-growing enterprise operating in the [INDUSTRY] sector. We specialize in delivering high-value solutions to our enterprise clients. Over the last three financial years, we have demonstrated consistent growth, expanding our operating revenue from ₹[REVENUE_FY1] Crores in FY1 to ₹[REVENUE_FY3] Crores in FY3.

2. COMPETITIVE STRENGTHS:
- Experienced Promoter and Management Team with deep domain expertise.
- Proven track record of financial profitability and operational efficiency.
- Scalable business model with robust client retention.

3. BUSINESS STRATEGY:
- Expanding geographical reach into Tier-2 and Tier-3 industrial hubs.
- Investing in R&D and proprietary technology platforms.
- Enhancing operating margins through process automation.

MANAGEMENT.precedent_template:
SECTION VIII: OUR MANAGEMENT & BOARD OF DIRECTORS

1. BOARD OF DIRECTORS:
In compliance with the Companies Act, 2013 and SEBI Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements (LODR) Regulations, our Board comprises an optimal combination of Executive, Non-Executive, and Independent Directors.

2. KEY MANAGERIAL PERSONNEL (KMP):
Our daily operations are spearheaded by our Promoter and Managing Director, supported by our Chief Financial Officer (CFO) and Company Secretary (CS) / Compliance Officer.

3. CORPORATE GOVERNANCE:
Our Company has constituted the mandatory Audit Committee, Nomination & Remuneration Committee, and Stakeholders' Relationship Committee.

FINANCIALS.precedent_template:
SECTION IX: FINANCIAL INFORMATION & RESTATED STATEMENTS

The restated summary financial statements of our Company for the preceding three financial years, audited by our Statutory Auditors in accordance with SEBI ICDR Regulations, are summarized below:

1. REVENUE FROM OPERATIONS:
- FY1: ₹[REVENUE_FY1] Crores
- FY2: ₹[REVENUE_FY2] Crores
- FY3: ₹[REVENUE_FY3] Crores

2. OPERATING PROFIT (EBITDA):
- FY1: ₹[EBITDA_FY1] Crores
- FY2: ₹[EBITDA_FY2] Crores
- FY3: ₹[EBITDA_FY3] Crores
(Satisfying SEBI ICDR Regulation 229 positive EBITDA requirement in at least 2 of 3 years)

3. PROFIT AFTER TAX (PAT) & NET WORTH:
- FY3 PAT: ₹[PAT_FY3] Crores | Net Worth: ₹[NET_WORTH] Crores.

LEGAL.precedent_template:
SECTION X: LEGAL & OTHER INFORMATION

1. OUTSTANDING LITIGATION & MATERIAL DEVELOPMENTS:
Except as disclosed in this section, there are no outstanding criminal proceedings, statutory or regulatory actions, or material civil litigations involving our Company, our Promoters, or our Directors that would materially impact our financial position.

2. GOVERNMENT & REGULATORY APPROVALS:
We have obtained all material statutory and regulatory licenses, permissions, and approvals necessary to carry out our normal business operations in India.

3. TAXATION MATTERS:
There are no material direct or indirect tax demands pending before any appellate authority except routine assessments.

GENERAL.precedent_template:
SECTION V: GENERAL INFORMATION

1. ISSUER DETAILS:
Company Name: [COMPANY_NAME]
CIN: [CIN]
Registered Office: [REGISTERED_OFFICE]

2. INTERMEDIARIES TO THE ISSUE:
Lead Manager: Merchant Banker (Lead Manager)
Legal Counsel: Legal Counsel / Advisor
Statutory Auditors: Chartered Accountants
Registrar to the Issue: Share Transfer Agent

3. AUTHORITY FOR THE ISSUE:
The present Issue has been authorized by a resolution of our Board of Directors and a Special Resolution passed by our shareholders under Section 62(1)(c) of the Companies Act, 2013.

PROMOTERS.precedent_template:
SECTION VII: OUR PROMOTERS & GROUP COMPANIES

1. PROMOTER BACKGROUND:
Our Company was promoted by our core founding promoters who possess over a decade of cumulative industry experience in the [INDUSTRY] sector.

2. PROMOTER SHAREHOLDING & CONTRIBUTION:
Prior to the public issue, our Promoters hold [PRE_ISSUE_SHARES] Equity Shares representing 100% of the pre-issue paid-up share capital. In compliance with Regulation 236 of SEBI ICDR Regulations, the Promoters shall contribute at least 20% of the post-issue share capital as Minimum Promoter's Contribution, which shall be locked in for a period of 3 years from the date of allotment.

3. GROUP COMPANIES:
Our Company does not have any material group companies with overlapping business interests that would cause a conflict of interest.

STATUTORY.precedent_template:
SECTION XI: STATUTORY & OTHER INFORMATION

1. MATERIAL CONTRACTS & DOCUMENTS FOR INSPECTION:
The copies of material contracts, including the Issue Agreement with the Lead Manager, Registrar Agreement, and Tripartite Agreements with Depositories, will be available for inspection at our Registered Office during normal business hours from the date of filing of this DRHP until the Issue Closing Date.

2. CONSENTS:
We have obtained written consents from our Lead Manager, Legal Counsel, Statutory Auditors, Registrar to the Issue, and Bankers to the Issue to act in their respective capacities.

OFFER_DETAILS.precedent_template:
SECTION XII: TERMS OF THE PRESENT ISSUE

1. ISSUE STRUCTURE:
Public Issue of up to [FRESH_ISSUE_SHARES] Equity Shares of Face Value ₹[FACE_VALUE]/- each for cash at a price of ₹[PRICE_BAND_HIGH]/- per Equity Share aggregating up to ₹[ISSUE_SIZE_CR] Crores.

2. MINIMUM APPLICATION SIZE:
In accordance with Regulation 231 of SEBI ICDR Regulations (2025 norm), applications by retail and institutional investors on the SME Exchange shall be for a minimum application value of ₹2,00,000/- (Two Lakh Rupees) and in multiples of the specified lot size thereafter.

3. BID / ISSUE TIMETABLE:
Bid/Issue Opens On: [TO_BE_NOTIFIED]
Bid/Issue Closes On: [TO_BE_NOTIFIED]
Finalization of Basis of Allotment: Within 2 working days of issue closure.

REGULATORY.precedent_template:
SECTION XIII: REGULATORY DISCLOSURES

1. SEBI ELIGIBILITY & COMPLIANCE:
Our Company is eligible for the Issue in accordance with Regulation 229 of Chapter IX of the SEBI (ICDR) Regulations, having a positive EBITDA in at least 2 of the 3 preceding financial years and a post-issue paid-up capital of ₹[POST_ISSUE_PAID_UP_CAPITAL] Crores (within the ₹1 Crore to ₹25 Crore SME limit).

2. PROHIBITION BY SEBI:
Neither our Company, nor our Promoters, nor our Directors are debarred or prohibited from accessing the capital markets by any order or direction passed by SEBI or any other regulatory authority.

3. WILFUL DEFAULTER / FRAUDULENT BORROWER:
Neither our Company nor our Promoters/Directors are categorized as a Wilful Defaulter or Fraudulent Borrower by any bank or financial institution as defined under RBI guidelines.

OFFER_STRUCTURE.precedent_template:
SECTION XIV: ISSUE STRUCTURE & ALLOCATION

1. ALLOCATION OF THE ISSUE:
The present 100% Book Built Issue is structured on the SME Platform of the Stock Exchange as follows:
- Qualified Institutional Buyers (QIBs): Not more than 50% of the Net Issue.
- Non-Institutional Investors (NIIs): Not less than 15% of the Net Issue.
- Retail Individual Investors (RIIs): Not less than 35% of the Net Issue.
- Market Maker Reservation Portion: 5% of the total Issue Size reserved exclusively for the Market Maker in compliance with Regulation 261 of SEBI ICDR Regulations.

2. ALLOTMENT PROCEDURE:
Allotment will be made in dematerialized form only through the ASBA (Application Supported by Blocked Amount) process.

APPLICATION_PROCEDURE.precedent_template:
SECTION XV: APPLICATION & ALLOTMENT PROCEDURE

1. MANDATORY ASBA & UPI:
All applicants must apply through the ASBA (Application Supported by Blocked Amount) process. Retail Individual Investors applying up to ₹5,00,000 can utilize the UPI (Unified Payments Interface) mechanism for blocking funds in accordance with SEBI circulars.

2. ELECTRONIC ALLOTMENT:
The Equity Shares will be allotted in dematerialized form only. Investors must ensure that their demat account is active and linked with a valid PAN and bank account prior to submitting the application.

We confirm that this Draft Red Herring Prospectus has been prepared in full compliance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and the SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018 (as amended up to 2025/2026).
